scene-01.text: Andani's eyes popped open before the sun was even up, and he lay still for a moment feeling two big feelings at the exact same time. He was so excited to go home to Chicago — to see Caleb and Jackson and grandma CeCe — but he also felt a little squeeze in his chest when he thought about leaving Cape Town, his friends, his room, and all the family who would wave goodbye at the door. Mom appeared in the doorway smiling, holding his little backpack open and said, 'Okay Andani — what do you want to pack?'

scene-02a.text: Andani ran straight to his bed and grabbed his favorite stuffed animal, hugging it once for good luck before tucking it carefully into his little backpack. Dad zipped it up and crouched down and said 'that's your travel buddy — he's going to help you be brave on the big plane.' Andani nodded very seriously, patted the backpack, and took one last long look around his room before heading for the door.

scene-02b.text: Andani ran straight to the kitchen and grabbed his favorite thing in the whole world — a big yellow banana — and carefully put it in his backpack first before anything else. Then Mom helped him tuck in a little bag of crackers for later, because bananas don't last forever and Chicago was very far away. Dad zipped the bag up and said 'smart packing buddy — a banana now and crackers for the long part.'

scene-03.text: Andani stood at the big check-in desk at Cape Town International Airport, just tall enough to peek over the counter at the friendly agent who was typing very fast on her keyboard. Mom and Dad lifted the big heavy suitcases onto the conveyor belt and Andani watched with huge eyes as they slowly disappeared through a little curtain — like magic! Then the agent leaned way over the counter and handed Andani his very own boarding pass to hold, and he gripped it in both hands like the most important piece of paper in the whole world.

scene-04a.text: Andani found a huge window overlooking the tarmac and pressed both hands flat against the cold glass, watching enormous planes move slowly back and forth in the early morning light like giant sleeping birds waking up. A ground crew member in a bright orange vest looked up from way down below and spotted Andani in the window — and waved right at him. Andani waved back so hard his whole arm went. Dad tapped his shoulder and said 'time to go find our gate, big guy.'

scene-04b.text: Andani and Mom wandered into the little airport shop and Andani's eyes went straight to a shelf full of small stuffed animals — lions, elephants, penguins — all wearing tiny shirts that said Cape Town. He picked up a little lion and turned it over in his hands, and Mom said 'do you want him to come to Chicago?' and Andani said yes please very quietly and very seriously. Dad found them at the checkout and laughed, and then it was time to find their gate.

scene-05.text: A friendly flight attendant with a big smile welcomed Andani at the door of the plane and said 'well hello there, are you the pilot?' which made Andani giggle. The plane inside was enormous — rows and rows of seats stretching all the way back. Mom pointed to their row and Andani looked at the two seats and had to make a very important decision.

scene-05a.text: Andani climbed over Dad and squeezed into the window seat, pressing his nose against the oval window to look out at Cape Town one last time. He buckled his own seatbelt — click — and looked up at Mom and Dad proudly. The engines began to hum, then roar, and the plane rolled faster and faster until suddenly the ground dropped away and Andani's tummy did a wonderful flip as Cape Town got smaller and smaller and the ocean spread out forever below like a giant blue carpet.

scene-05b.text: Andani sat in the aisle seat and watched everything happening up and down the plane — flight attendants checking bags, passengers finding their seats, a baby across the aisle waving at him. A friendly flight attendant crouched down beside him and pinned a pair of tiny silver wings onto his shirt and said 'those are for brave first-time long-distance flyers.' Andani looked down at his wings and sat up very straight indeed. Then the engines roared and suddenly they were in the air.

scene-06.text: Once the plane was up in the clouds a flight attendant came by with a little activity pack and handed it to Andani — inside were six crayons, a small coloring book, and some blank white paper. Andani spread everything out on his tray table very carefully like a professional. Mom smiled and said 'what do you want to make first, Andani?'

scene-06a.text: Andani grabbed the green crayon first and started drawing the biggest T-Rex he could fit on the paper, with tiny arms, enormous stomping feet, and teeth like mountains. Dad peeked over and declared it the most fearsome dinosaur he had ever seen in his life, so Andani added a little figure on the dinosaur's back and said 'that's you Dad' which made everyone in the nearby seats laugh. He was just adding a pterodactyl in the corner when the plane suddenly lurched sideways and the seatbelt light pinged on.

scene-06b.text: Andani flipped through the coloring book until he found the perfect page — a big friendly elephant standing under a tree — and he got to work immediately and very seriously. He gave the elephant a purple trunk because purple was his favorite color, and orange ears because orange was his second favorite, and a yellow tummy just because. He was just starting on the tree when the plane suddenly lurched and the seatbelt light pinged on.

scene-07.text: The plane shook and rattled and bumped like it was driving over an invisible rocky road high up in the sky, and a few people around them said 'ooooh!' Mom buckled Andani in tight and took his hand, and Dad leaned over in his calmest voice and said 'it's just turbulence buddy — like driving over a speed bump, it's completely okay.' Andani held Mom's hand and watched Dad's face very carefully, and because Dad looked completely calm, Andani decided to be completely calm too. Then slowly the shaking stopped, and the pilot said 'smooth skies ahead' — and everyone on the plane let out one big relieved breath.

scene-07a.text: Andani picked his favorite movie on the little screen in front of him and snuggled back into his seat, his travel buddy tucked under one arm, feeling much better already. The plane hummed quietly all around him as it flew high above the dark Atlantic Ocean, carrying him closer and closer to Chicago with every single minute. He was so cozy and the movie was so good that his eyes started getting very heavy, and before long he was fast asleep with a small smile on his face.

scene-07b.text: Andani pressed his face back against the window and looked out at the most incredible clouds he had ever seen — great white towers rising up like castles, and flat ones stretched out like snowy roads, and wispy ones that looked like someone had pulled cotton apart with their fingers. Dad pointed out that far below all those clouds was the Atlantic Ocean — the biggest stretch of water in the whole world — and somewhere on the other side of it was Chicago.

scene-08.text: The plane had been flying for a long long time and the sky outside had gone from blue to orange to black to blue again. A flight attendant appeared with a tray of airplane food — a little bread roll, some pasta, a tiny pot of fruit, and a cookie — and set it on Andani's tray table. Mom said 'what do you think, Andani — hungry or sleepy?'

scene-08a.text: Andani inspected his airplane meal very carefully before trying anything — he sniffed the pasta, poked the fruit, and squeezed the bread roll to check it was real. Then he tried a bite of pasta and decided it was pretty good actually, and the fruit was very good, and the bread roll was excellent, and the cookie was absolutely perfectly the best thing he had ever eaten on an airplane. Dad stole a bite of the cookie and Andani did not appreciate that at all.

scene-08b.text: Andani curled up in his seat with his travel buddy pressed against his cheek and closed his eyes while the plane hummed its low steady hum all around him. He dreamed about Caleb and Jackson chasing him around grandma CeCe's garden, and about grandma CeCe's hugs that smelled like flowers and felt like the warmest blanket in the world. Then through his dream he heard Mom's voice, soft and close, whispering 'Andani... baby... wake up... we're almost in Chicago.'

scene-09.text: Andani sat up and rubbed his eyes and looked out the window — and there below the clouds was America, spread out like a patchwork quilt of roads and fields and neighborhoods, and right there on the edge of it all was a great shimmering sheet of water and a cluster of tall silver buildings reaching up into the sky. Mom said 'Andani, look — that's Chicago!' and he pressed both hands against the window and stared.

scene-09a.text: Andani spotted the water first — an enormous blue lake so big it looked like the ocean, stretching all the way to the horizon with no other shore in sight. 'That's a LAKE?' Andani said, because he had seen lakes before and they were nothing like this. Dad laughed and said Lake Michigan was one of the biggest lakes in the whole world, and Andani made a mental note to tell Caleb and Jackson that he had seen it from the sky. Then the plane banked and his ears went pop pop pop.

scene-09b.text: Andani spotted it immediately — the Willis Tower, taller than everything around it, standing straight and serious above the Chicago skyline just like in the photos Caleb and Jackson had sent him. 'I SEE IT!' Andani shouted, and the passengers around them smiled. Mom said 'that's near where your cousins live' and Andani pressed his nose flat against the glass and didn't move until the plane banked away and his ears went pop pop pop.

scene-10.text: The wheels hit the runway with a great bump and a squeal and the whole plane roared as it slowed down, and Andani gripped his armrests and grinned the whole time because it felt like the world's best carnival ride. Mom helped Andani with his backpack and Dad said 'okay buddy — we're in Chicago. Ready?' and Andani said 'READY' so loud the people in front of them turned around and laughed.

scene-10a.text: The moment they were through the gate Andani broke into a full run, his backpack bouncing, his sneakers squeaking on the shiny floor, weaving between all the tall grown-up legs with expert skill. Dad caught up and scooped him up mid-run and carried him the rest of the way zooming him through the terminal like a little airplane, making engine noises, while Mom followed behind laughing and pulling the luggage cart. They burst through a set of big double doors — and there they were.

scene-10b.text: Andani walked slowly through O'Hare with his head swiveling in every direction — moving walkways, enormous colorful murals, and a long tunnel with a ceiling that glowed and shifted through every color of the rainbow while music played. Andani stopped completely in the middle of the rainbow tunnel and stood there with his mouth open until Dad gently took his hand and said 'some very special people are waiting.' They pushed through a set of big double doors — and there they were.

scene-11.text: Dad pulled the car into grandma CeCe's street and before he had even finished parking Andani could see the front door flying open and Caleb and Jackson tumbling out onto the porch in their socks. Andani was unbuckled before Mom could even turn around, and the three cousins met in the front yard in one big crashing hug that ended with all of them on the grass laughing. Grandma CeCe appeared in the doorway in her apron with her arms open wide and said 'get in here all of you — the pizza is getting cold' and that was the best sentence Andani had heard in his entire life.

scene-final.text: That night the whole family crowded around grandma CeCe's big round table with a deep dish pizza in the middle so tall and gooey and golden that Andani's eyes went wide as dinner plates. He had never tasted anything like it — the cheese stretched in long strings and the sauce was rich and warm. Around the table everyone talked and laughed and told stories, and Andani looked from face to face — Mom, Dad, Caleb, Jackson, grandma CeCe — and felt the biggest warmest fullest feeling he had ever felt in his whole chest. Later that night Caleb and Jackson fell asleep on either side of him on grandma CeCe's big couch, and Andani lay there in the quiet with his travel buddy tucked under his chin, thinking that of all the places in the world, this one — right here, right now — was his favorite.
